title: cassia rotundata var. grandistipula
intro: Cassia rotundata var. grandistipula, also known as African senna, is a plant species in the legume family, scientifically recognized for its medicinal properties and potential health benefits. It is traditionally used as a natural laxative, due to its high concentration of anthraquinones, which stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation. The herbal preparations of Cassia rotundata var. grandistipula include tea, capsules, and extracts, often used to treat various gastrointestinal issues and promote overall digestive health. However, its use should be cautious, as excessive consumption can lead to gastrointestinal irritation and electrolyte imbalance, underscoring the need for proper dosing and medical supervision.
